trade.summary: India exports pharmaceuticals, engineering goods, chemicals, textiles, and IT services to South Africa. South Africa exports precious metals and minerals (gold, platinum, chromium), coal, and vehicles. India runs a trade surplus. South Africa is India's largest trading partner in Africa. The India-Southern African Customs Union (SACU) trade relationship is growing.

diplomatic.summary: India and South Africa have a 'Strategic Partnership' with deep historical ties. Both nations were connected through Gandhi's experiences in South Africa, which shaped his philosophy. Both are founding IBSA members (India-Brazil-South Africa) and BRICS members. PM Modi and South African presidents have met at multiple multilateral forums. South Africa is India's gateway to the African continent.

military.summary: Growing defence cooperation. India has provided defence equipment (BrahMos missile interest, corvettes). Joint naval exercises (IBSAMAR) as part of the IBSA trilateral framework. India-South Africa defence committee meets regularly.

financial.summary: Indian companies (Mahindra, Tata, Cipla, Dr Reddy's) have significant presence in South Africa. South Africa's mining sector attracts Indian investment. Indian pharmaceutical generics are critical for South Africa's healthcare system. Indian IT companies serve South African corporate clients.

energy.summary: South Africa imports Indian refined petroleum products. India imports some South African coal. Cooperation on renewable energy transitions is growing. India and South Africa co-lead some BASIC (Brazil-South Africa-India-China) climate negotiations.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation through IBSA, BRICS, and bilateral agreements. Joint research in health (HIV/AIDS, TB), space science (ISRO-SANSA cooperation), and astronomy (SALT telescope). South African universities host Indian academic collaborations. SKA (Square Kilometre Array) radio telescope in South Africa involves Indian scientific contributions.
